categories.name: Open Elective
categories.description: Any EAS / CIS / ESE / ENGR 5000–9999 online course

courses.title: Computer Systems Programming
courses.notes: C / systems programming. C++ background from COS CSCI 001/002 (A+) covers pointers & memory — the hardest part. New areas: system calls, processes, threads, file I/O.

courses.title: Algorithms and Computation
courses.notes: Graduate-level algorithms. Strong foundation from SJSU CS 146 Data Structures & Algorithms (A). Grad rigor adds complexity analysis and proof techniques.

courses.title: Fundamentals of Linear Algebra and Optimization
courses.notes: Linear algebra covered in SJSU MATH 39 (A). Optimization part is new but builds naturally on existing math background.

courses.title: Mathematical Foundations for ML I: Probability
courses.alt_note: Alternative to CIS 5150. Pair with EAS 5170 to satisfy 1 CU requirement.
courses.notes: Alternative foundation path. Solid background from SJSU MATH 161A/B Applied Probability & Statistics.

courses.title: Mathematical Foundations for ML II: Linear Algebra
courses.alt_note: Alternative to CIS 5150. Pair with EAS 5160 to satisfy 1 CU requirement.
courses.notes: Alternative foundation path. Strong linear algebra background from SJSU MATH 39 (A).

courses.title: Big Data Analytics
courses.notes: Spark / Hadoop ecosystem. Background from SJSU CS 131 Processing Big Data (A). Graduate version carries heavier engineering project load.

courses.title: Database and Information Systems
courses.notes: SQL + relational theory + system design. CS 157A at SJSU was the weakest grade (B−). Dedicated summer term allows focused catch-up.

courses.title: Machine Learning for Data Science
courses.notes: Graduate ML — more mathematical than undergrad. Background from CS 171 Intro ML (A). Sequenced after Stats + Linear Algebra for best results.

courses.title: Statistics for Data Science
courses.notes: Strong foundation from SJSU MATH 161A/B Applied Probability & Statistics (A / A+). Graduate rigor is elevated but very manageable.

courses.title: Algorithms for Big Data
courses.notes: Theory-heavy. Streaming algorithms, sketching, randomized methods applied to massive data. Requires strong algorithm background.

courses.title: Artificial Intelligence
courses.notes: Solid background from SJSU CS 156 Intro AI (A+). Covers search, logic, planning, probabilistic inference systematically.

courses.title: Natural Language Processing
courses.notes: Extends ML background into text / language. Fast-moving field — Transformer-era content requires staying current.

courses.title: GPU Computing for Machine Learning Systems
courses.notes: CUDA programming + GPU architecture. High systems-level knowledge required. Best taken after CIT 5950.

courses.title: Computer Vision & Computational Photography
courses.notes: Vision algorithms + deep learning for images. Good extension of ML and Python background.

courses.title: Principles of Deep Learning
courses.notes: High math demand (matrix calculus, backprop from scratch). Large project load. Strong ML background helps significantly.

courses.title: Technology Ethics and the Legal Landscape
courses.notes: Reading and writing focused. Excellent pairing with a harder technical course. Strong writing background (COMM 001 A+, PHIL 025 A+).

courses.title: How to Use Data
courses.notes: Applied and case-study based. Low weekly load — ideal buffer course to pair with heavier technical work. 0.5 CU.

courses.title: Imaging Informatics
courses.notes: Clinical and medical imaging focus. 0.5 CU.

courses.title: Medical Image Analysis
courses.notes: Deep learning applied to medical images. Pairs well with EAS 5850. 0.5 CU.

courses.title: Generative AI for Virtual Environments
courses.notes: GenAI applications in virtual / augmented reality contexts. Emerging field. 0.5 CU.

courses.title: Data Science Capstone
courses.notes: End-to-end DS project integrating all coursework. Strong option as a final-semester capstone course.

courses.title: Open Elective
courses.notes: Any eligible online course in EAS, CIS, ESE, or ENGR at the 5000–9999 level. Course TBD based on interests at the time.
